title: Use purged rolling-origin validation as the model-selection gate, not just grouped random folds
summary: The new tyre-state paper evaluates with rolling-origin CV, and scikit-learn’s `TimeSeriesSplit` exists precisely because future-to-past evaluation can mislead.
rationale: The new tyre-state paper evaluates with rolling-origin CV, and scikit-learn’s `TimeSeriesSplit` exists precisely because future-to-past evaluation can mislead. Here, race strategy patterns drift by season and even by event, so shuffled grouped CV can reward brittle race memorization and overstate gains from high-cardinality or opponent-aware features.
implementation_hint: Order races chronologically by `Year` and weekend, then evaluate forward-only folds with a gap or full-race purge. Keep your current grouped CV for variance estimates, but accept a new feature/model only if it improves both grouped CV and forward CV. Use forward-CV OOF predictions for blend-weight tuning.
expected_effect: Fewer false positives in research iterations and better alignment between local CV and leaderboard/generalization.
risk: Scores may drop initially because the validation is harsher; some genuinely useful race-specific priors may look worse under forward splits if train coverage is thin.
